docs.title: Welcome to Apaleo API Client
docs.text: A powerful, async-first Python client library for the Apaleo API, providing seamless integration with Apaleo's comprehensive hospitality and travel management platform.

docs.title: What is Apaleo API Client?
docs.text:
The Apaleo API Client is a production-ready Python library that wraps the Apaleo Swagger API, offering both synchronous and asynchronous access to Apaleo's features and functionalities. Built with modern Python best practices, it provides:

  • 🔐 OAuth2 Authentication - Automatic token management with refresh capabilities
  • ⚡ Async/Await Support - Built for high-performance concurrent operations
  • 📝 Type Safety - Comprehensive Pydantic models for all requests and responses
  • 🛡️ Error Handling - Domain-specific exceptions with detailed error information
  • 📄 Pagination - Smart concurrent batch fetching for large datasets
  • 🔧 Extensible - Modular adapter pattern supporting all Apaleo API domains

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Get started with Apaleo API Client in just a few lines:

from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient

# Initialize the client
client = ApaleoAPIClient(
    client_id="your-client-id",
    client_secret="your-client-secret"
)

# Fetch properties asynchronously
properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties()
print(f"Found {properties.count} properties")

# Clean up
await client.aclose()

docs.title: Comprehensive API Coverage
docs.text: Supports all major Apaleo API domains: - Core API - Properties, inventory, rates, and units management - Identity API - OAuth2 authentication and user management - Payment API - Payment processing and transaction handling - Webhook API - Event-driven integrations - Integration API - Third-party system connections - Fiscalization API - Tax and compliance management - Profile API - User profile and preference management

docs.title: Developer Experience
docs.text:
• Type Complete - Full type hints and IDE autocompletion
  • Async Native - Built on httpx for optimal performance
  • Testing Ready - Comprehensive test coverage and dry-run mode
  • Well Documented - Detailed API reference and examples

docs.title: Production Ready
docs.text:
• Robust Error Handling - Typed exceptions for all HTTP status codes
  • Automatic Retries - Configurable retry logic for transient failures
  • Rate Limiting - Built-in concurrent request management
  • Logging Integration - Comprehensive logging for debugging and monitoring

docs.title: Supported APIs
docs.text:
The client provides access to these Apaleo API endpoints:

API Domain Base URL Status Core https://api.apaleo.com/ ✅ Implemented Identity https://identity.apaleo.com/ ✅ Implemented Payment https://payment.apaleo.com/ 🔧 In Progress Webhook https://webhook.apaleo.com/ 🔧 In Progress Integration https://integration.apaleo.com/ 📋 Planned Fiscalization https://fiscalization.apaleo.com/ 📋 Planned Profile https://profile.apaleo.com/ 📋 Planned

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
Apaleo Core Inventory API Adapter.

This module contains the implementation of the InventoryPort interface to interact with the Apaleo Inventory API.

See: https://api.apaleo.com/swagger/index.html?urls.primaryName=Inventory+V1

docs.title: CoreInventoryPort
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Source code in src/apaleoapi/apaleo/core/v1/apis/inventory.py 
class CoreInventoryPort(Protocol):
    _path: str = "inventory"
    _version: str

    # Property methods

    async def list_properties(self, params: PropertyListParams | None = None) -> PropertyList:
        """List properties with optional filters."""
        pass

    async def create_property(self, idempotency_key: str) -> Any:
        """Create a new property."""
        pass

    async def get_property(
        self, property_id: str, params: PropertyGetParams | None = None
    ) -> Property:
        """Get property details by ID."""
        pass

    # Types methods

    async def list_countries(self) -> CountryList:
        """List available countries."""
        pass

docs.title: CoreV1InventoryAdapter
docs.text:
Bases: CoreInventoryPort

Source code in src/apaleoapi/apaleo/core/v1/apis/inventory.py 
class CoreV1InventoryAdapter(CoreInventoryPort):
    def __init__(self, transport: AsyncTransportPort, max_concurrent: int, dry_run: bool = False):
        self._t = transport
        self._version = "v1"
        self._dry_run = dry_run
        self._response_handler = ResponseHandler()
        self._response_validator = ResponseValidator()
        self._list_fetcher = ListFetcher(max_concurrent=max_concurrent)
        self._base_path = f"{self._path}/{self._version}"

    # Property methods

    async def _list_properties(self, params: PropertyListParamsModel) -> PropertyListModel:
        """
        Helper method to list properties with optional filters, returning validated response model.
        """
        url = f"{self._base_path}/properties"

        async def _fetch_data(params: PropertyListParamsModel) -> PropertyListModel:
            params_dict = params.model_dump(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True)
            response = await self._t.request("GET", url, params=params_dict)
            response_data = self._response_handler.handle(response)
            return self._response_validator.validate(
                response_data=response_data,
                response=response,
                model_cls=PropertyListModel,
                error_prefix="Invalid property list payload from Apaleo inventory",
            )

        return await self._list_fetcher.fetch(
            fetch_fn=_fetch_data,
            params=params,
            model_cls=PropertyListModel,
        )

    async def list_properties(self, params: PropertyListParams | None = None) -> PropertyList:
        """
        List properties with optional filters.

        Args:
            params (PropertyListParams, optional):
                Filters and pagination options for listing properties.

                Supported fields:
                    - status (str):
                        Filter properties by status. Allowed values: "Test", "Live".

                    - include_archived (bool):
                        Whether to include archived properties in the results.
                        If omitted or False, only non-archived properties are returned.

                    - country_code (str):
                        Filter properties by ISO country code (e.g., "US", "DE").

                    - expand (list[str]):
                        List of embedded resources to expand in the response.
                        Supported values: "actions". All other values are ignored.

                    - page_number (int):
                        Page number to retrieve. Default: 1.
                        If the requested page contains no items, the API returns
                        HTTP 204 (No Content).

                    - page_size (int):
                        Number of items per page. Default: 500. Maximum: 500.

                    - batch_size (int):
                        Number of items fetched per request when retrieving pages
                        concurrently.

                    - is_concurrently (bool):
                        Whether pages should be fetched concurrently. Default: False.

        Returns:
            PropertyList:
                A list of properties matching the provided filters.
        """
        params_dict: dict[str, Any] = asdict(params) if is_dataclass(params) else {}
        params_model = PropertyListParamsModel.model_validate(params_dict)
        validated_response = await self._list_properties(params=params_model)
        return from_dict(data_class=PropertyList, data=validated_response.model_dump())

    async def create_property(self, idempotency_key: str) -> Any:
        raise NotImplementedError("Create property method is not implemented yet.")

    async def _get_property(
        self, property_id: str, params: PropertyGetParamsModel
    ) -> PropertyModel:
        """Helper method to get property details by ID, returning validated response model."""
        url = f"{self._base_path}/properties/{property_id}"
        params_dict = params.model_dump(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True)
        response = await self._t.request("GET", url, params=params_dict)
        response_data = self._response_handler.handle(response)
        return self._response_validator.validate(
            response_data=response_data,
            response=response,
            model_cls=PropertyModel,
            error_prefix="Invalid property item payload from Apaleo inventory",
        )

    async def get_property(
        self, property_id: str, params: PropertyGetParams | None = None
    ) -> Property:
        """Get property details by ID."""
        params_dict: dict[str, Any] = asdict(params) if is_dataclass(params) else {}
        params_model = PropertyGetParamsModel.model_validate(params_dict)
        if self._dry_run:
            fake_response = PropertyFactory().build()
            return cast(Property, fake_response)
        validated_response = await self._get_property(property_id=property_id, params=params_model)
        return from_dict(data_class=Property, data=validated_response.model_dump())

    # Types methods

    async def _list_countries(self) -> CountryListModel:
        """
        Helper method to list available countries, returning validated response model.
        """
        url = f"{self._base_path}/types/countries"
        response = await self._t.request("GET", url)
        response_data = self._response_handler.handle(response)
        return self._response_validator.validate(
            response_data=response_data,
            response=response,
            model_cls=CountryListModel,
            error_prefix="Invalid country list payload from Apaleo inventory",
        )

    async def list_countries(self) -> CountryList:
        """
        List available countries.
        """
        validated_response = await self._list_countries()
        return from_dict(data_class=CountryList, data=validated_response.model_dump())
        return from_dict(data_class=CountryList, data=validated_response.model_dump())

async def list_properties(self, params: PropertyListParams | None = None) -> PropertyList:
    """
    List properties with optional filters.

    Args:
        params (PropertyListParams, optional):
            Filters and pagination options for listing properties.

            Supported fields:
                - status (str):
                    Filter properties by status. Allowed values: "Test", "Live".

                - include_archived (bool):
                    Whether to include archived properties in the results.
                    If omitted or False, only non-archived properties are returned.

                - country_code (str):
                    Filter properties by ISO country code (e.g., "US", "DE").

                - expand (list[str]):
                    List of embedded resources to expand in the response.
                    Supported values: "actions". All other values are ignored.

                - page_number (int):
                    Page number to retrieve. Default: 1.
                    If the requested page contains no items, the API returns
                    HTTP 204 (No Content).

                - page_size (int):
                    Number of items per page. Default: 500. Maximum: 500.

                - batch_size (int):
                    Number of items fetched per request when retrieving pages
                    concurrently.

                - is_concurrently (bool):
                    Whether pages should be fetched concurrently. Default: False.

    Returns:
        PropertyList:
            A list of properties matching the provided filters.
    """
    params_dict: dict[str, Any] = asdict(params) if is_dataclass(params) else {}
    params_model = PropertyListParamsModel.model_validate(params_dict)
    validated_response = await self._list_properties(params=params_model)
    return from_dict(data_class=PropertyList, data=validated_response.model_dump())

docs.title: Changelog All notable changes to the Apaleo API Client will be documented in this file. The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning. ## [Unreleased] ### Added - Initial release of Apaleo API Client - OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication flow - Core API v1 Inventory support - Async-first architecture with httpx - Type-safe Pydantic models for all requests/responses - Comprehensive error handling with domain-specific exceptions - Automatic pagination with concurrent batch fetching - Built-in retry logic with exponential backoff - Dry-run mode for testing and development - Complete type hints and IDE support - Extensive test coverage with pytest - Documentation with MkDocs and MkDocs Material ### API Coverage - ✅ Core API v1: Property, inventory, and rates management - list_properties() - List properties with filtering - get_property(id) - Get property details - list_countries() - Get available countries - create_property() - Create new properties - 🔧 Identity API: OAuth2 token management (partial) - 📋 Payment API: Payment processing (planned) - 📋 Webhook API: Event handling (planned) - 📋 Integration API: Third-party integrations (planned) - 📋 Fiscalization API: Tax compliance (planned) - 📋 Profile API: User profiles (planned) ### Features - Authentication - OAuth2 Client Credentials Provider with automatic token refresh - Secure credential handling - Token caching and expiry management - HTTP Transport - Async HTTP client based on httpx - Automatic retries with configurable backoff - Rate limiting with concurrent request management - Request/response logging and debugging - Data Handling - Pydantic v2 models for validation - Dataclasses for domain objects - Automatic serialization/deserialization - Type-safe parameter construction - Error Management - Typed exceptions for HTTP status codes: - BadRequestError (400) - UnauthorizedError (401) - ForbiddenError (403) - NotFoundError (404) - ValidationError (422) - InternalServerError (500) - ServiceUnavailableError (503) - Detailed error messages with context - Pagination - Smart concurrent batch fetching - Configurable page sizes and batch sizes - Memory-efficient streaming for large datasets - Progress tracking and error recovery - Development Tools - Dry-run mode for safe testing - Comprehensive logging configuration - Test factories with Polyfactory - Integration test support ### Dependencies - Runtime: - httpx>=0.28.1 - Async HTTP client - pydantic>=2.12.5 - Data validation - dacite>=1.9.2 - Dataclass conversion - httpx-retries>=0.4.6 - Retry middleware - polyfactory>=3.3.0 - Test factories - Development: - pytest>=7.0 - Testing framework - pytest-asyncio>=0.21 - Async test support - ruff>=0.1.0 - Linting and formatting - mypy>=1.0 - Type checking - coverage>=7.0 - Coverage reporting - invoke>=2.0 - Task automation - nox>=2023.4.22 - Multi-environment testing ### Requirements - Python: 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13 - OS: Linux, macOS, Windows - Architecture: x86_64, ARM64 (Apple Silicon) ### Documentation - Complete API documentation with mkdocstrings - User guides and tutorials - Installation and setup instructions - Development and contributing guidelines - Troubleshooting and help sections - Logo and branding for documentation site ### Testing - 95%+ test coverage - Unit tests with mocked dependencies - Integration tests with Apaleo API - Contract tests for API compliance - Performance benchmarks - Multi-Python version testing with nox ### Code Quality - Strict mypy type checking - Ruff linting with comprehensive rules - Black-compatible formatting - Import sorting with isort - Pre-commit hooks for quality gates --- ## Release Notes Format For future releases, we'll follow this format: ## [1.0.0] - YYYY-MM-DD ### Added - New features and capabilities ### Changed - Changes to existing functionality ### Deprecated - Features marked for removal ### Removed - Features removed in this release ### Fixed - Bug fixes ### Security - Security-related improvements --- ## Version Policy We follow Semantic Versioning: - MAJOR (X.0.0): Breaking changes to public APIs - MINOR (0.X.0): New features, backwards compatible - PATCH (0.0.X): Bug fixes, backwards compatible ### Breaking Changes We consider these changes as breaking: - Removing or renaming public methods/classes - Changing method signatures (parameters, return types) - Changing exception types or error handling behavior - Requiring new dependencies - Dropping Python version support ### Backwards Compatibility We maintain backwards compatibility for: - Public API methods and classes - Configuration options and environment variables - Error types and error handling patterns - Data model structures ### Deprecation Policy 1. Announcement: Deprecated features are marked with warnings 2. Grace Period: Minimum 2 minor versions before removal 3. Documentation: Clear migration guides provided 4. Examples: Updated to show new recommended approaches --- ## Planned Releases ### v1.1.0 (Planned) - Payment API v1 support - Webhook API v1 support - Enhanced error handling and retry logic - Performance optimizations - Additional Core API endpoints ### v1.2.0 (Planned) - Integration API v1 support - Profile API v1 support - Advanced authentication methods - Caching layer for responses - Metrics and monitoring integration ### v1.3.0 (Planned) - Fiscalization API v1 support - Real-time event streaming - GraphQL support (if available) - Advanced pagination strategies - Plugin system for extensions --- View releases on GitHub

Contributing to Apaleo API Client We welcome contributions to the Apaleo API Client! Whether you're fixing bugs, adding features, improving documentation, or helping with testing, your contributions are valued. ## 🚀 Quick Start for Contributors ### 1. Set Up Development Environment bash # Fork the repository on GitHub first, then clone git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/apaleo-api-client-python.git cd apaleo-api-client-python # Install with all development dependencies poetry install --with dev,test,lint,docs # Activate the virtual environment poetry shell # Verify installation inv help ### 2. Check Everything Works bash # Run tests inv test # Run linting inv lint # Format code inv format # Build documentation inv docs ### 3. Make Your Changes bash # Create a feature branch git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name # Make your changes # ... # Run tests and linting inv test inv lint # Commit your changes git add . git commit -m "feat: add your feature description" # Push to your fork git push origin feature/your-feature-name ### 4. Submit a Pull Request 1. Go to the GitHub repository 2. Click "New Pull Request" 3. Select your branch 4. Fill out the PR template 5. Wait for review feedback ## 📄 Project Structure Understanding the codebase structure: src/apaleoapi/ ├── __init__.py # Package exports ├── client.py # Main ApaleoAPIClient ├── constants.py # API endpoints and constants ├── exceptions.py # Custom exception classes ├── logging.py # Logging configuration ├── schemas.py # Pydantic schema definitions ├── typing.py # Type definitions │ ├── apaleo/ # API domain modules │ ├── core/ # Core API (properties, inventory) │ ├── identity/ # Identity & authentication │ ├── payment/ # Payment processing │ ├── webhook/ # Webhook management │ ├── integration/ # Third-party integrations │ ├── fiscalization/ # Tax and compliance │ └── profile/ # User profiles │ ├── http/ # HTTP layer │ ├── auth.py # OAuth2 authentication │ └── transport.py # HTTP transport layer │ └── services/ # Support services ├── list_fetcher.py # Pagination handling ├── response_handler.py # Response processing └── response_validator.py # Response validation ## 🗺️ Development Workflow ### Available Tasks (via invoke) bash inv help # Show all available tasks # Development inv test # Run tests with pytest inv test-nox # Test across Python 3.10-3.13 inv test-unit # Run unit tests only inv test-integration # Run integration tests only # Code Quality inv lint # Run all linters (ruff, mypy, etc.) inv format # Format code (ruff format, isort) inv check # Check code quality without fixing # Documentation inv docs # Build and serve documentation inv docs-build # Build documentation only inv docs-clean # Clean generated docs # Building inv build # Build package inv clean # Clean build artifacts ### Testing Workflow #### Unit Tests bash # Run all unit tests pytest tests/ -m unit # Run specific test file pytest tests/test_client.py # Run with coverage pytest tests/ --cov=apaleoapi --cov-report=html #### Integration Tests bash # Run integration tests (requires API credentials) APALEO_CLIENT_ID=test APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET=test pytest tests/ -m integration #### Test Categories We use pytest markers to categorize tests: - @pytest.mark.unit - Fast, isolated unit tests - @pytest.mark.integration - Tests that call real APIs - @pytest.mark.contract - API contract validation tests ### Code Style We use these tools for code quality: - ruff - Fast Python linter and formatter - mypy - Static type checking - isort - Import sorting - pytest - Testing framework #### Code Style Rules - Line length: 100 characters - Use type hints for all public APIs - Follow PEP 8 and Black formatting - Docstrings for all public functions/classes - Use dataclasses for structured data ## 🔥 Adding New Features ### Adding a New API Domain 1. Create directory structure: bash mkdir -p src/apaleoapi/apaleo/newdomain/v1/{apis,dataclasses,schemas} touch src/apaleoapi/apaleo/newdomain/__init__.py touch src/apaleoapi/apaleo/newdomain/v1/__init__.py 2. Create the adapter: python # src/apaleoapi/apaleo/newdomain/v1/apis/example.py from apaleoapi.services.list_fetcher import ListFetcher from apaleoapi.services.response_handler import ResponseHandler class NewDomainV1ExampleAdapter: def __init__(self, transport, list_fetcher, response_handler): self._transport = transport self._list_fetcher = list_fetcher self._response_handler = response_handler async def list_items(self, params=None): # Implement your API call pass 3. Add to main client: python # src/apaleoapi/client.py from .apaleo.newdomain.v1.apis.example import NewDomainV1ExampleAdapter class ApaleoAPIClient: def __init__(self, ...): # ... self.new_domain = self._create_new_domain_adapter() def _create_new_domain_adapter(self): return NewDomainV1ExampleAdapter( transport=self._transport, list_fetcher=self._list_fetcher, response_handler=self._response_handler ) ### Adding Dataclasses and Schemas 1. Create Pydantic schemas: python # src/apaleoapi/apaleo/newdomain/v1/schemas/example.py from pydantic import BaseModel, Field from typing import Optional class ItemListParamsModel(BaseModel): page_size: Optional[int] = Field(default=50, ge=1, le=100) page_number: Optional[int] = Field(default=1, ge=1) 2. Create dataclasses: python # src/apaleoapi/apaleo/newdomain/v1/dataclasses/example.py from dataclasses import dataclass from typing import Optional @dataclass class ItemListParams: page_size: Optional[int] = 50 page_number: Optional[int] = 1 ### Writing Tests #### Unit Test Example python import pytest from unittest.mock import Mock, AsyncMock from apaleoapi.apaleo.newdomain.v1.apis.example import NewDomainV1ExampleAdapter class TestNewDomainAdapter: def setup_method(self): self.transport = AsyncMock() self.list_fetcher = Mock() self.response_handler = Mock() self.adapter = NewDomainV1ExampleAdapter( transport=self.transport, list_fetcher=self.list_fetcher, response_handler=self.response_handler ) @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_list_items_success(self): # Setup mocks expected_response = {"items": [], "count": 0} self.response_handler.handle.return_value = expected_response # Execute result = await self.adapter.list_items() # Verify assert result == expected_response self.transport.get.assert_called_once() #### Integration Test Example python import pytest from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient @pytest.mark.integration class TestNewDomainIntegration: @pytest.fixture async def client(self): client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id="test-id", client_secret="test-secret", dry_run=True # Use dry_run for safe testing ) yield client await client.aclose() @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_list_items_integration(self, client): result = await client.new_domain.v1.example.list_items() assert hasattr(result, 'items') assert hasattr(result, 'count') ## 📖 Documentation ### API Documentation We use mkdocstrings to auto-generate API docs from docstrings: python class ExampleAdapter: """Adapter for Example API operations. This adapter provides access to example-related functionality in the Apaleo API. Attributes: base_url: The base URL for example API endpoints. Example: python adapter = client.new_domain.v1.example items = await adapter.list_items() """ async def list_items(self, params: Optional[ItemListParams] = None) -> ItemList: """List all items with optional filtering. Args: params: Optional filtering and pagination parameters. Returns: ItemList: A paginated list of items. Raises: ValidationError: If parameters are invalid. NotFoundError: If no items are found. Example: python from apaleoapi.apaleo.newdomain.v1.dataclasses import ItemListParams params = ItemListParams(page_size=10) items = await adapter.list_items(params) print(f"Found {items.count} items") """ # Implementation here pass ### User Guide Documentation Add examples to the appropriate documentation files: - Update docs/api.md for API reference - Add examples to user guides - Update README.md if adding major features ## 🐛 Bug Fixes ### Bug Report Workflow 1. Reproduce the bug: python # Create minimal reproduction case import asyncio from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient async def reproduce_bug(): client = ApaleoAPIClient(client_id="test", client_secret="test") # Steps to reproduce... await client.aclose() asyncio.run(reproduce_bug()) 2. Write a failing test: python def test_bug_reproduction(): # Test that demonstrates the bug pass 3. Fix the bug: - Make minimal changes to fix the issue - Ensure existing tests still pass - Add regression test 4. Verify the fix: bash inv test inv lint ## 📅 Release Process (For maintainers) ### Version Bumping We use semantic versioning: - MAJOR: Breaking API changes - MINOR: New features, backwards compatible - PATCH: Bug fixes, backwards compatible bash # Update version in pyproject.toml poetry version patch|minor|major # Update CHANGELOG.md # Git commit and tag git add . git commit -m "chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z" git tag vX.Y.Z git push origin main --tags ### Release Checklist - [ ] All tests pass across Python 3.10-3.13 - [ ] Documentation is up to date - [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated - [ ] Version is bumped in pyproject.toml - [ ] Git tag matches package version - [ ] GitHub release is created - [ ] Package is published to PyPI ## 🚀 Advanced Contributing ### Performance Contributions - Use asyncio profiling for async code - Benchmark before/after changes - Consider memory usage for large datasets - Test with various network conditions ### Security Contributions - Never log sensitive information (credentials, tokens) - Use environment variables for test credentials - Follow OWASP guidelines for HTTP clients - Consider timing attacks in authentication ### API Design Principles - Favor async methods for I/O operations - Use type hints for all public APIs - Make required parameters explicit - Provide sensible defaults for optional parameters - Use dataclasses for complex parameters - Handle errors gracefully with typed exceptions ## 📉 Commit Message Format We follow Conventional Commits: <type>([scope]): <description> [optional body] [optional footer(s)] Types: - feat: A new feature - fix: A bug fix - docs: Documentation changes - style: Code style changes (formatting, etc.) - refactor: Code changes that neither fix bugs nor add features - test: Adding or updating tests - chore: Maintenance tasks Examples: feat(core): add property search functionality fix(auth): handle token refresh race condition docs: update installation guide for Poetry users test(integration): add tests for pagination edge cases --- ## 💙 Thank You! Every contribution helps make Apaleo API Client better for everyone. Whether you're fixing a typo, adding a feature, or helping with documentation, your efforts are appreciated! Questions? Don't hesitate to: - Open a discussion - Ask in issues - Reach out to the maintainers Happy coding! 🚀

Help with Apaleo API Client Need help with the Apaleo API Client? This guide covers common issues, troubleshooting steps, and where to get support. ## 🔍 Common Issues & Solutions ### Authentication Problems #### Invalid Client Credentials Symptoms: - UnauthorizedError (401) on all API calls - "Invalid client credentials" error message Solutions: 1. Verify credentials: python # Double-check your credentials print(f"Client ID: {client_id}") print(f"Client Secret: {client_secret[:4]}...") 2. Check credential format: python # Ensure no extra spaces or characters client_id = client_id.strip() client_secret = client_secret.strip() 3. Validate in Apaleo Developer Portal: - Log into developer.apaleo.com - Verify your application status - Regenerate credentials if needed #### Token Expiration Issues Symptoms: - Intermittent UnauthorizedError during long-running operations - "Token expired" messages Solution: The client handles token refresh automatically, but you can force refresh: python # Force token refresh await client._transport._token_provider.get_token(force_refresh=True) ### Connection & Network Issues #### Timeout Errors Symptoms: - httpx.TimeoutException - Operations taking too long Solutions: 1. Increase timeout: python client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id=client_id, client_secret=client_secret, timeout=60.0 # Increased from default 30s ) 2. Use async context manager: python async with ApaleoAPIClient(...) as client: # Operations here pass # Client automatically closed #### SSL/Certificate Issues Symptoms: - SSLError or certificate verification failures - "Certificate verify failed" messages Solutions: 1. Update certificates: bash # On Ubuntu/Debian sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install ca-certificates # On macOS brew install ca-certificates 2. Corporate proxy/firewall: python import httpx # Configure proxy if needed client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id=client_id, client_secret=client_secret, transport_kwargs={ "proxies": "http://proxy.company.com:8080", "verify": "/path/to/corporate/ca.pem" } ) ### Rate Limiting & Performance #### Rate Limit Exceeded Symptoms: - TooManyRequestsError (429) - "Rate limit exceeded" messages Solutions: 1. Reduce concurrent requests: python client = ApaleoAPIClient( ..., max_concurrent=2 # Reduced from default ) 2. Add delays between requests: python import asyncio for property_id in property_ids: property_data = await client.core.v1.inventory.get_property(property_id) await asyncio.sleep(0.1) # 100ms delay 3. Use pagination wisely: python # Smaller page sizes, concurrent fetching params = PropertyListParams( page_size=25, # Smaller pages is_concurrently=True, batch_size=50 # Moderate batch size ) #### Memory Issues with Large Datasets Symptoms: - MemoryError when fetching large datasets - Progressive slowdown during pagination Solutions: 1. Use iterative processing: python # Process pages individually instead of getting all at once page = 1 while True: params = PropertyListParams(page_number=page, page_size=100) result = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params) if not result.items: break # Process this page for property in result.items: # Handle each property pass page += 1 2. Disable concurrent fetching for very large datasets: python params = PropertyListParams( is_concurrently=False, # Sequential processing page_size=50 ) ### Data & Type Issues #### Validation Errors Symptoms: - ValidationError with field details - "Field required" or "Invalid value" messages Solutions: 1. Check API documentation: python # Use Pydantic validation to understand expected format from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import PropertyCreateRequest # See what fields are required print(PropertyCreateRequest.__annotations__) 2. Use factory classes for testing: python from polyfactory import AsyncFactory from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import PropertyListParams class PropertyParamsFactory(AsyncFactory[PropertyListParams]): __model__ = PropertyListParams # Generate valid test data test_params = PropertyParamsFactory.build() ## 🔎 Debugging & Logging ### Enable Debug Logging python from apaleoapi.logging import setup_logging # Enable detailed logging logger = setup_logging( level="DEBUG", format_string="%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s" ) # Now you'll see: # - HTTP request/response details # - Authentication token flows # - Pagination logic # - Error stack traces ### HTTP Request/Response Inspection python import httpx # Log all HTTP traffic transport = httpx.AsyncHTTPTransport( retries=3, # Add logging for requests event_hooks={ "request": [lambda request: print(f"Request: {request.method} {request.url}")], "response": [lambda response: print(f"Response: {response.status_code}")] } ) client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id=client_id, client_secret=client_secret, transport=transport ) ### Dry Run Mode for Testing python # No actual API calls made client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id="dummy", client_secret="dummy", dry_run=True ) # Returns empty/default responses properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties() print(properties.count) # 0 print(properties.items) # [] ## 🔧 Development Setup Issues ### Poetry Installation Problems bash # Clean Poetry cache poetry cache clear --all pypi # Reinstall dependencies poetry install --with dev,test,lint,docs # Update Poetry itself curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 - ### Python Version Compatibility bash # Check Python version python --version # Apaleo API Client requires Python 3.10+ # Install with pyenv if needed: pyenv install 3.12.0 pyenv local 3.12.0 ### Import Issues python # If imports fail, verify installation: pip list | grep apaleo # Check installation location: import apaleoapi print(apaleoapi.__file__) # Reinstall if needed: pip uninstall apaleo-api-client pip install apaleo-api-client ## 📊 Performance Optimization ### Optimize for Your Use Case 1. High-throughput scenarios: python client = ApaleoAPIClient( max_concurrent=10, # More concurrent requests retries=1, # Fewer retries timeout=15.0 # Shorter timeout ) 2. Stable network, fewer errors: python client = ApaleoAPIClient( max_concurrent=5, # Moderate concurrency retries=3, # More retries timeout=60.0 # Longer timeout ) 3. Unreliable network: python client = ApaleoAPIClient( max_concurrent=2, # Lower concurrency retries=5, # Many retries timeout=120.0 # Very long timeout ) ## 📞 Getting Support ### Community Support 1. GitHub Issues - Report bugs - Request features - Ask questions 2. Stack Overflow - Tag your questions with apaleo and python - Include minimal reproducible examples - Check existing questions first ### When Reporting Issues Include this information: python # System information import sys import platform from apaleoapi import __version__ print(f"Python version: {sys.version}") print(f"Platform: {platform.platform()}") print(f"Apaleo API Client version: {__version__}") # Error details # - Full error traceback # - Minimal code to reproduce # - Expected vs. actual behavior # - Any relevant log output ### Commercial Support For enterprise customers: - Contact your Apaleo account manager - Professional services available - SLA-backed support options ## 💵 API Limits & Quotas Understand Apaleo API limits: | Resource | Limit | Notes | |----------|-------|-------| | Requests per minute | 1000 | Per application | | Concurrent connections | 10 | Per client | | Response size | 50MB | Maximum | | Timeout | 30s | Default | --- Still need help? 🚀 Open an issue or join our discussions!

Installation Get started with Apaleo API Client quickly and easily. The library supports Python 3.10+ and can be installed using your preferred package manager. ## 📦 Package Installation ### Using pip (recommended) bash pip install apaleo-api-client ### Using Poetry bash poetry add apaleo-api-client ### Using pipenv bash pipenv install apaleo-api-client ### Using conda bash conda install -c conda-forge apaleo-api-client ## 🔧 Development Installation For development or contributing to the project: ### Clone the Repository bash git clone https://github.com/apaleo/apaleo-api-client-python.git cd apaleo-api-client-python ### Install with Poetry (Recommended) bash # Install with all development dependencies poetry install --with dev,test,lint,docs # Activate the virtual environment poetry shell ### Install with pip bash # Create virtual environment python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate # Install in development mode pip install -e ".[dev,test,lint,docs]" ## 🔍 System Requirements ### Python Version - Python 3.10 or higher - Python 3.13 is fully supported - Tested on CPython (PyPy support coming soon) ### Operating Systems - ✔️ Linux (Ubuntu, CentOS, Alpine) - ✔️ macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon) - ✔️ Windows (10, 11, Server) ### Dependencies Core dependencies (automatically installed): httpx>=0.28.1 # Async HTTP client pydantic>=2.12.5 # Data validation and serialization dacite>=1.9.2 # Dataclass conversion httpx-retries>=0.4.6 # HTTP retry middleware polyfactory>=3.3.0 # Testing factories ## 🗂️ Verify Installation Test that the installation was successful: python from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient from apaleoapi import __version__ print(f"Apaleo API Client version: {__version__}") # Test client initialization (dry-run mode) client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id="test", client_secret="test", dry_run=True ) print("Installation successful!") ## 🏡 Getting API Credentials Before using the client, you'll need Apaleo API credentials: ### 1. Register with Apaleo 1. Visit the Apaleo Developer Portal 2. Create a developer account or sign in 3. Navigate to "Applications" or "API Keys" ### 2. Create an Application 1. Click "Create Application" 2. Enter your application details: - Name: Your application name - Description: Brief description of your integration - Redirect URI: For OAuth flows (if needed) ### 3. Get Your Credentials After creating the application, you'll receive: - Client ID: Public identifier for your application - Client Secret: Private secret (keep secure!) ### 4. Configure Scopes Select the appropriate API scopes for your use case: - core:read - Read access to Core API - core:write - Write access to Core API - identity:read - Read access to Identity API - Additional scopes as needed ## ⚙️ Configuration ### Environment Variables Store your credentials securely using environment variables: bash # .env file APALEO_CLIENT_ID=your-client-id-here APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET=your-client-secret-here APALEO_TIMEOUT=30 APALEO_MAX_RETRIES=3 APALEO_MAX_CONCURRENT=5 Load them in your application: python import os from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_ID"), client_secret=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET"), timeout=float(os.getenv("APALEO_TIMEOUT", 30)), retries=int(os.getenv("APALEO_MAX_RETRIES", 3)), max_concurrent=int(os.getenv("APALEO_MAX_CONCURRENT", 5)) ) ### Configuration File Alternatively, use a configuration file: yaml # apaleo_config.yaml apaleo: client_id: "your-client-id" client_secret: "your-client-secret" timeout: 30.0 retries: 3 max_concurrent: 5 dry_run: false logging: level: "INFO" format: "%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s" python import yaml from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient with open("apaleo_config.yaml") as f: config = yaml.safe_load(f) client = ApaleoAPIClient(**config["apaleo"]) ## 🛡️ Security Best Practices ### Credential Management - ⚠️ Never commit credentials to version control - Use environment variables or secure secret management - Rotate credentials regularly - Use different credentials for development/staging/production ### Network Security - Always use HTTPS (enforced by default) - Consider using a VPN for sensitive environments - Monitor API access logs ### Application Security - Validate all user inputs before API calls - Implement proper error handling - Log security events appropriately - Follow the principle of least privilege for API scopes ## 🔥 Quick Start Example Put it all together with a complete example: python import asyncio import os from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import PropertyListParams async def main(): # Initialize client with environment variables client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_ID"), client_secret=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET") ) try: # Fetch first 10 properties params = PropertyListParams(page_size=10) properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params) print(f"Found {properties.count} total properties") for prop in properties.items: print(f"- {prop.name} ({prop.code})") finally: # Always clean up await client.aclose() if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(main()) ## 🔄 Upgrading ### Check Current Version python from apaleoapi import __version__ print(__version__) ### Upgrade to Latest bash pip install --upgrade apaleo-api-client ### Upgrade with Poetry bash poetry update apaleo-api-client ## 🎆 Next Steps Now that you have the library installed: 1. Learn the basics - Understand key concepts 2. Explore examples - See practical usage patterns 3. Read the API docs - Dive into the full API reference 4. Join the community - Get help and contribute --- Having issues with installation? Check our troubleshooting guide or open an issue.

API Reference Comprehensive API documentation for the Apaleo API Client. This page provides detailed information about all classes, methods, and data models. ## Core Client ### ApaleoAPIClient ::: apaleoapi.client.ApaleoAPIClient options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ## Authentication ### OAuth2 Providers ::: apaleoapi.http.auth options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ## Core API ### Inventory Management ::: apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.apis.inventory options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ### Core Data Models #### Query Parameters ::: apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory.query options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 4 #### Response Objects ::: apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory.response options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 4 ## HTTP Layer ### Transport ::: apaleoapi.http.transport options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ## Services ### Response Handling ::: apaleoapi.services.response_handler options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ### List Fetching & Pagination ::: apaleoapi.services.list_fetcher options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ### Response Validation ::: apaleoapi.services.response_validator options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ## Exceptions ### Exception Hierarchy ::: apaleoapi.exceptions options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ### Domain-Specific Exceptions ::: apaleoapi.apaleo.exceptions options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ## Configuration ### Constants ::: apaleoapi.constants options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ### Logging ::: apaleoapi.logging options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 ## Type Definitions ::: apaleoapi.typing options: show_root_heading: true show_source: false heading_level: 3 --- ## Quick Reference ### Basic Usage Pattern python import asyncio from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import PropertyListParams async def main(): # Initialize client client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id="your-client-id", client_secret="your-client-secret" ) try: # Use the API params = PropertyListParams(page_size=10) properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params) print(f"Found {properties.count} properties") for prop in properties.items: print(f" - {prop.name}") finally: # Clean up await client.aclose() if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(main()) ### Error Handling Pattern python from apaleoapi.exceptions import ( ValidationError, NotFoundError, UnauthorizedError ) try: property = await client.core.v1.inventory.get_property("property-id") except NotFoundError: print("Property not found") except UnauthorizedError: print("Invalid credentials") except ValidationError as e: print(f"Invalid request: {e.message}") ### Configuration Pattern python from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient from apaleoapi.logging import setup_logging # Configure logging logger = setup_logging(level="DEBUG") # Configure client client = ApaleoAPIClient( client_id=client_id, client_secret=client_secret, timeout=30.0, # Request timeout retries=3, # Retry attempts max_concurrent=5, # Concurrent requests dry_run=False # Disable for production ) ### Pagination Pattern python from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import PropertyListParams # Efficient pagination with concurrent fetching params = PropertyListParams( page_size=50, # Items per page batch_size=100, # Concurrent batch size is_concurrently=True # Enable concurrent fetching ) properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params) # Manual pagination page = 1 while True: params = PropertyListParams(page_number=page, page_size=100) result = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params) if not result.items: break # Process this page for property in result.items: print(property.name) page += 1 --- ## Data Models Reference ### Common Patterns All data models follow consistent patterns: #### Query Parameters - Use *Params classes (e.g., PropertyListParams) - Optional fields with sensible defaults - Type validation with Pydantic - Pagination support built-in #### Response Objects - Use *List classes for paginated responses - Use *Detail classes for single items - Rich metadata (counts, pagination info) - Nested object support #### Request Bodies - Use *Request classes for create/update operations - Required vs. optional field validation - Automatic serialization to JSON - Support for partial updates ### Type Safety All public APIs include comprehensive type hints: python from typing import Optional from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import ( PropertyListParams, PropertyList, PropertyDetail ) # Type-safe parameter construction params: PropertyListParams = PropertyListParams( page_size=50, name="Hotel*" ) # Type-safe response handling result: PropertyList = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params) first_property: PropertyDetail = result.items[0] # Type hint available --- For more examples and tutorials, see our User Guide
Why use Apaleo API Client?
docs.text: The Apaleo API Client provides a modern, developer-friendly way to integrate with Apaleo's hospitality platform. Here's why you should choose it over direct API calls or other integration methods.

docs.title: Async-First Architecture
docs.text:
• Built on httpx for high-performance concurrent operations
  • Native async/await support for non-blocking I/O
  • Concurrent request batching for pagination
# Fetch multiple pages concurrently
properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(
    PropertyListParams(is_concurrently=True, batch_size=100)
)

docs.title: Smart Pagination
docs.text:
• Automatic pagination handling with concurrent fetching
  • Configurable batch sizes for optimal performance
  • Built-in memory management for large datasets

docs.title: Comprehensive Error Handling
docs.text:
• Typed exceptions for all HTTP status codes
  • Detailed error messages with context
  • Graceful handling of API rate limits and transient failures
from apaleoapi.exceptions import ValidationError, NotFoundError

try:
    property = await client.core.v1.inventory.get_property("invalid-id")
except NotFoundError:
    print("Property not found")
except ValidationError as e:
    print(f"Invalid request: {e.message}")

docs.title: Automatic Token Management
docs.text:
• OAuth2 Client Credentials flow with automatic refresh
  • Token caching and expiry tracking
  • Seamless handling of 401 responses

docs.title: Built-in Retries
docs.text:
• Configurable retry logic for transient failures
  • Exponential backoff with jitter
  • Respect for Retry-After headers

docs.title: Complete Type Coverage
docs.text:
• Full type hints for all methods and responses
  • Pydantic models for request/response validation
  • IDE autocompletion and error detection
# Type-safe parameter construction
params = PropertyListParams(
    page_number=1,        # int
    page_size=50,        # int  
    name="Hotel*",       # Optional[str]
    city="Berlin"        # Optional[str]
)

# Typed response handling
properties: PropertyList = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params)
for property: PropertyDetail in properties.items:
    print(property.name)  # IDE knows this is a str

docs.title: Rich Data Models
docs.text:
• Structured dataclasses for all domain objects
  • Automatic serialization/deserialization
  • Validation with detailed error messages

docs.title: Dry-Run Mode
docs.text:
Perfect for development and testing:

# No actual API calls made
client = ApaleoAPIClient(..., dry_run=True)
properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties()
# Returns empty PropertyList for testing

docs.title: Comprehensive Logging
docs.text:
• Detailed request/response logging
  • Configurable log levels
  • Integration with Python's standard logging module
from apaleoapi.logging import setup_logging

logger = setup_logging(level="DEBUG")
# See all HTTP requests, responses, and timing information

docs.title: Testing Support
docs.text:
• Mock-friendly design with dependency injection
  • Async test patterns with pytest-asyncio
  • Factory classes for test data generation

docs.title: Production Deployment
docs.text:
• Configurable timeouts and connection limits
  • Memory-efficient streaming for large responses
  • Monitoring and observability hooks

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Secure credential handling
  • TLS/SSL verification by default
  • No credential logging in production mode

docs.title: Scalability
docs.text:
• Concurrent request limiting to respect API quotas
  • Efficient connection pooling
  • Horizontal scaling support

docs.title: vs. Direct HTTP Calls
docs.text: \u2714\ufe0f No manual token management \u2714\ufe0f Built-in error handling and retries \u2714\ufe0f Type safety and validation \u2714\ufe0f Pagination automation \u2714\ufe0f Consistent API interface

docs.title: vs. Generic API Clients
docs.text: \u2714\ufe0f Domain-specific error types \u2714\ufe0f Apaleo-optimized request patterns \u2714\ufe0f Built-in knowledge of API quirks \u2714\ufe0f Specialized data models \u2714\ufe0f Hotel industry domain knowledge

docs.title: vs. Custom Wrappers
docs.text: \u2714\ufe0f Production-tested reliability \u2714\ufe0f Comprehensive test coverage \u2714\ufe0f Active maintenance and updates \u2714\ufe0f Community support and documentation \u2714\ufe0f Performance optimizations

docs.title: Property Management Systems (PMS)
docs.text:
• Sync property data and availability
  • Manage rates and inventory
  • Handle reservations and guest information

docs.title: Channel Managers
docs.text:
• Distribute inventory across multiple channels
  • Real-time rate and availability updates
  • Central reservation management

docs.title: Revenue Management
docs.text:
• Analyze booking patterns and performance
  • Dynamic pricing optimization
  • Competitive market analysis

docs.title: Business Intelligence
docs.text:
• Extract data for reporting and analytics
  • Monitor KPIs and performance metrics
  • Historical trend analysis

docs.title: Integration Platforms
docs.text:
• Connect Apaleo with other hospitality systems
  • Data synchronization between platforms
  • Workflow automation

Ready to experience the benefits? Install Apaleo API Client \u2192
